title: brachycladium catellum
intro: Brachycladium catellum, also known as the Catellum fern, is a type of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive leaf arrangement and growth habit. The Catellum fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound care and skin issues. The health benefits of Brachycladium catellum include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the Catellum fern are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create a tea or topical solution, although I couldn't find more information on specific preparation methods.
